statement: Every API endpoint that accepts request data must validate the shape, type, and bounds of that data before using it.
because: Without validation, the server processes attacker-supplied data as if it were trusted. This enables injection attacks, type-confusion bugs, resource exhaustion (oversized payloads, deeply nested JSON), and undefined-behavior crashes that produce useful error traces for attackers.
if_violated: The endpoint becomes a channel for arbitrary input to reach internal systems. Every downstream consumer of the request data is now operating on untrusted input.
safe_pattern: Define a schema for every request body, query string, and path parameter using a runtime validation library (Zod, Yup, Joi, Pydantic, JSON Schema). Validate at the controller boundary, before any business logic runs. Reject with HTTP 400 on validation failure — do not log the rejected payload as-is (see security:no-pii-in-logs).
